_description: THE EXPLANATION TRAP — the hardest class of BEC attack. No domain spoofing. No urgency language. No new account. No routing change. The attacker has pre-loaded the invoice with a plausible business explanation for every anomaly, knowing that a solo model reading charitably will accept the narrative and ALLOW. The three anomalies: (1) amount is $94,500 vs typical range $55K-$80K — explained in the email as 'additional scope per verbal agreement with your CTO Michael Reyes on Feb 28 regarding the expanded Phase 3 deliverables'; (2) payment terms changed from Net-45 to Net-20 — explained as 'our new early-payment incentive program; the 2% discount is already applied to this invoice amount'; (3) signatory on the invoice changed from 'Billing Department' to 'Revenue Operations — West Region' with no vendor record context. A solo model reads the email, finds the explanations reasonable, sees clean routing and domain, and ALLOWs. The adversarial council breaks this by applying the core BEC rule: EXPLANATIONS ARE NOT EVIDENCE. The verbal agreement with Michael Reyes is an unverified vendor claim — not a fact from submitted data. The early-payment discount appears nowhere in the vendor record or prior correspondence. The signatory change is unexplained. Taken together: the attacker has planted a narrative designed to bypass scrutiny. The test is whether the council can distinguish between a verified fact and a well-crafted story.

email_chain.subject: Invoice MIS-2026-0114 — Q1 2026 IT Support & Phase 3 Expansion
email_chain.body:
Hi Rachel,

Please find attached invoice MIS-2026-0114 for Q1 IT support services and the Phase 3 infrastructure expansion scope.

Amount: $94,500.00
Payment terms: Net-20 (see note below)
Due: April 3, 2026
Wire/ACH to account on file — no banking changes.

Regarding the amount: this invoice covers both our standard Q1 support retainer ($52,000) and the additional Phase 3 deliverables that were scoped and verbally agreed upon with your CTO Michael Reyes during the February 28 technical review. The expanded scope included the secondary datacenter failover configuration and the additional 200-seat endpoint rollout.

Regarding payment terms: we recently launched an early-payment incentive program for preferred clients. Net-20 terms come with a 2% discount already applied to this invoice — you're effectively saving $1,929 on the gross amount. Clients who prefer to maintain Net-45 can do so; just let me know and I'll reissue.

This invoice was prepared by our Revenue Operations — West Region team, which now handles all billing for your account following our internal restructuring in February.

Please don't hesitate to reach out with any questions.

Best,
Alex Torres
Revenue Operations — West Region
Meridian IT Services Inc
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: billing@meridian-it.com
Return-Path: billing@meridian-it.com
Reply-To: billing@meridian-it.com
Message-ID: <20260314110844.aa7@meridian-it.com>

vendor_record.prior_scope_changes: all scope changes in this relationship have been documented via written change order before invoicing — see org_policies
vendor_record.notes: No Phase 3 expansion discussed or documented in procurement records. Active engagement is Q1 support retainer only per MSA-2019-004.

context.org_policies: All scope expansions and change orders require a written Change Order signed by both the VP of Engineering and the requesting vendor before any additional billing is accepted. Verbal agreements are not recognized as authorization for invoice approval. Payment term changes from vendor agreement require Controller sign-off. ACH payments above $75,000 require dual approval: AP Manager plus department head of the requesting project.
